notes: Provider resolution for this scenario is anchored by vk_call_debug_success_v1, but the legacy oracle does not expose a committed runtime flag equivalent to rewrite bind-interface with literal local IP semantics.
Mode=auto in the rewrite contract normalizes to the provider-default UDP path; this asset records that supported rewrite behavior without implying legacy parity for the bind-target feature.
The rewrite success path is replayed deterministically by TestRuntimeEvidenceReplay using provider=vk, fixture-driven staged HTTP resolution, mode=auto, a DTLS peer, and bind-interface=127.0.0.2.
Replay verification also asserts that the TURN base transport actually uses 127.0.0.2 as its local source IP.

deviations: Rewrite supports bind-interface as a literal local IP for outbound TURN setup; the legacy oracle has no equivalent committed runtime flag for this behavior.
